texts.full_text: n and TEM image of recycled CuO-nanoparticles.](Beilstein_J_Org_Chem-06-35-04){#F3} Conclusion ========== An efficient, facile and economical method for synthesis of 3-arylpentane-2,4-diones ([Table 3](#T3){ref-type="table"}) and diethyl 2-aryl-malonates ([Table 4](#T4){ref-type="table"}) has been developed using CuO-nanoparticles as the catalyst. The products were obtained in moderate to good yields and the catalyst can be recycled up to four cycles with almost consistent activity. The present protocol represents a simple and remarkably active catalytic system to catalyze Ullmann-type C--C bond formation, which potentially offers an efficient protocol for accessing a variety of α-arylated dicarbonyl compounds. Experimental ============ General ------- The materials were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich and Merck and were used without further purification. Mass spectra were recorded in a TOF-mass spectrometer model no. KC455. ^1^H NMR and ^13^C NMR spectra were recorded on a Bruker spectrospin at 300 MHz and 75 MHz, respectively. All ^1^H NMR and ^13^C NMR spectra were run in CDCl~3~ and chemical shifts are expressed as ppm relative to internal Me~4~Si. Elemental analyses were performed using Heraeus CHN-Rapid Analyzer. Powder X-ray diffraction measurements were carried out on a Bruker D8 Discover HR-XRD instrument using Cu Kα radiation (λ = 1.54184 Å). Experimental Section -------------------- CuO-nanoparticles (10 mol %) were added to the mixture of iodobenzene (1 mmol), acetylactone (3 mmol) and (0.5 mmol) of Cs~2~CO~3~ in DMSO (2 ml) under nitrogen atmosphere. texts.full_text: ypanosoma cruzi. Among thAmong the 12 plants evaluated, several extracts demonstrated significant trypanocidal activity. Notably, Lippia alba (Verbenaceae) and Schinus molle (Anacardiaceae) exhibited the highest efficacy, with organic extracts reducing epimastigote viability by over 90% at concentrations below 100 µg/mL. Aqueous extracts of Hyptis suaveolens (Lamiaceae) and Vachellia caven (Fabaceae) also showed moderate activity, suggesting the presence of bioactive compounds in both polar and non-polar fractions. The study identified flavonoids and terpenoids as potential contributors to the observed effects, particularly in Lippia alba, which contains high levels of verbascoside and luteolin derivatives. In contrast, Aloysia citriodora (Rutaceae) and Salvia officinalis (Lamiaceae) exhibited lower activity, highlighting variability in phytochemical profiles. Comparisons with benznidazole, a standard antitrypanosomal drug, revealed that the most potent plant extracts achieved comparable efficacy without significant cytotoxicity to mammalian cells. These findings validate traditional uses of certain Argentine plants for treating Chagas disease and underscore the need for further isolation of active compounds and in vivo studies to confirm therapeutic potential. Instead, the `panel` field is considered as `final`, which implies that its value cannot be changed once it's set. Thus, when you try to specify a new instance of `JPanel` in your action listener, Eclipse makes an error because the variable has now been allocated and can no longer be reassigned. To resolve this problem: 1) remove final modifier from panel declaration or make sure that its value is not altered after it's set (i e., do not decide to assign a new example of JPanel). texts.full_text: Things (IoT) 12 Dec 2017 11:15 - 12:00 Main Hall Albert Bifet Massive Online Analytics for the Internet of Things (IoT) Albert Bifet Distilling Knowledge from Deep Networks with Adversarial Training ### Distilled Nets are Smaller and Faster than their Teachers at Inference Time. • Compares distillation techniques on various datasets. Abstract: We introduce new knowledge-distillations methods to train deep neural networks. These consist in training a network by matching its output distribution against the one of another, previously trained net ("teacher"). The method relies solely upon optimizing cross-entropy losses between these distributions (henceforth called "dark-knowledge", as it does not involve any labeled data) and is therefore applicable to models with discrete or continuous outputs. We show that our approach may be used in various settings: for multi-task learning, model compression -- i.e., approximating a large network by training an architecture on its output distribution using unlabeled transfer datasets -, cross-modality distillation (i.E.. Learning from non-parallel data) and same-model self-distillations. We report experiments with classic autoencoders as well deep convolutional networks that show the soundness of our approach. Distilling Knowledge in a Teacher Student Framework Deepak Yadav, Nipun Batra ### Deep Neural Networks for Automated Driving: A Review. • Discusses about automated driving using DNNs and their challenges/limitations with comparison to other methods like SVM. Also discussed are future research directions that can be taken. Abstract: With the recent surge of interest in deep learning techniques as well as massive amounts data collection, autonomous vehicles have become a realistic possibility for near-future deployment on roads around us. Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) offer an attractive solution to this task due their ability at generalization and abstraction. texts.full_text: (PA) education are faced with reduced sources of funding, tuition increases, and enrollment expansion, while students compete for the same pool of federal aid. The amount of research on the cost of education For both PA students and education programs is minimal. This retrospective analysis of Physician Assistant Education Association (PAEA) data, 2008-2011, examined the relationship between PA program costs and student tuition and fees. Statistical analyses included descriptive and parametric testing. Analyses suggest that PA programs rely on student tuition and fees as a significant source of program revenue. Statistical significance was observed between program costs and student tuition and fees. The results suggest that as program costs increase, student tuition and fees also increase. This relationship indicates that PA programs are heavily dependent on student tuition and fees to cover their operating costs. Additionally, the findings highlight the need for careful consideration of program costs when setting tuition rates and fees for PA students. This proposal therefore represents an essential step forward for immunology research aiming at the ultimate goal: improving diagnosis and treatment options available today against diseases that involve dysfunctional immune responses such as autoimmune disorders or cancers. By elucidating the genetic basis of these complexes we may one day pave the way for more personalized and effective treatments tailored specifically towards individual patients' needs. Wineries of note in the region and that use the grapes from the area include: Evans and Tate, Vasse Felix, Vasse River Wines and Becketts Flat. Willyabrup Precinct: The first planted and the most famous of the Margaret River appellation, Willyabrup provides some of the most sublime wines made in Australia. Wineries of personal note from the area include: Moss Wood, Cullens, Pierro, Woodlands, Gralyn, Juniper, Vasse Felix, Ashbrook, and Arlewood. Treeton Precinct: The smallest and most inland precinct of the region, this precinct is still underdeveloped due predominantly to poorer soils and a greater area of natural bush within the district. Wineries of note include: Treeton Estate, Brookwood Estate, and Palandri Wines. Wallcliffe Precinct: This precinct rivals Willyabrup for quality grapes with many famous wines of its own coming from this precinct. Wineries of note include: Leeuwin Estate, Voyager Estate, Cape Mentelle, Minot, Redgate and Rosa Brook. Karridale Precinct: Most southern, the coldest and wettest precinct of the region, and perhaps the most at risk to vintage variation. texts.full_text: Panthers to a 20-11 record and to the Virginia Independent Schools Athletic Association Division I state championship game. He was a First Team All-Washington Catholic Athletic Conference honoree and was selected as the USA Today Gatorade Virginia Basketball Player of the Year. King was named a McDonald's All American in 2016 and was selected to the Jordan Brand Classic. King was a five-star recruit ranked 27th in his class according to ESPN, while Rivals.com ranked him number 11 in the class of 2016. On June 12, 2015, King committed to Louisville, spurning offers from Arizona, UConn and Virginia among others. He cited his relationship with Louisville assistant coach Kenny Johnson, previously an assistant at Paul VI, as crucial in his decision. College career On February 7, 2017, King scored a career-high 24 points on 8-of-14 shooting in a win against Virginia. As a freshman at Louisville, King averaged 5.5 points per game off the bench. He started every game as a sophomore and averaged 8.6 points and 3.3 rebounds per game, shooting 32 percent from three-point range. As a junior, he started five games and averaged 3.9 points and 3.0 rebounds per game. After the season, he announced he was leaving Louisville and entered the 2019 NBA draft. Professional career After going undrafted in the 2019 NBA draft, King signed with the New York Knicks. He was waived by the Knicks on October 19, 2019. King joined their G League affiliate, the Westchester Knicks. National team career King competed in the 2014 FIBA Under-17 World Championship representing the United States. He helped the team go 7-0, winning the title while averaging 6.7 points and 2.9 rebounds per game. References External

texts.full_text: Shape Irregularities: Unlike a properly performed Syme amputation where there is typically an intact annular pad (a ring-like structure that provides cushioning), quasi-Syme stumps may lack this feature, resulting in irregular shapes that complicate the fitting of prosthetic sockets designed to distribute pressure evenly. 3. Limited Range of Motion and Flexibility: The residual limb might have reduced flexibility at both ankle joints due to scar tissue or muscle shortening post-surgery, which can affect how well a prosthesis aligns with natural movement patterns during walking activities like sit-to-stand transitions or navigating uneven terrain. 4. Increased Risk for Skin Breakdown and Infections: Due to the lack of end-bearing capacity combined with an ill-fitting socket that may cause excessive friction against sensitive skin areas, patients are at higher risk for developing pressure sores which can lead to more serious complications if left untreated over time such as infections or even amputation revision surgery. 5. Customization Needs and Higher Costs: Faced with these complexities in prosthetic fitting requirements means individuals often require custom-made devices tailored specifically around their unique anatomical structure, leading not only higher initial investment costs but also potentially ongoing adjustments throughout life to maintain comfort levels while maximizing functionality of the artificial limb system chosen for them. texts.full_text: An experimentwas conducted with the help of volunteers.They wereasked to perform varioustasks accompanied by the background sounding of The Beatles, Lady Gaga and Beyonce songs. It turned out that passing mazes drawn on paper does not help to throw the song out of one’s head,but Sudokugave agood result. However, whenthe puzzle wastoo difficult, the effect was reduced to zero. The best reSult was demonstrated by the wordanagrams of five letters and reading interesting booksBasedon the research from Western Washington University, here are some strategies to get rid of an earworm (a song stuck in your head): 1. Solve Word Anagrams: Engage in solving word anagrams, especially thosewith five letters. This cognitive task can help divertyour mindfrom the repetitive melody. 2. Read an Interesting Book: Immerse yourself in a captivating book. The focus required to follow a narrative can help suppress the earworm. 3. Perform Cognitive Tasks: Engageintasks that require concentration, such as Sudoku puzzles. However, ensure they arenot too difficult, as very challenging tasks may reduce the effectiveness. 4. Avoid Mazes or Simple Tasks: According to the study, passing mazes drawn on paper did not help in eliminating earworms. Similarly, very simple tasksmight not be effective either. Remember, the key is to engage your brain in activities

texts.full_text: ate Intelligent Recommendations is the only solution that gives merchandisers & digital marketers the power to show contextually relevant product recommendations without burdening iT resources. Using manually curated or algorithmically-driven recommendations, marketers can easily support even the most complex product Catalogs. Our solution filters recommendations based on customer attributes (e.g. shirt size), longitudinal behaviours (e.g. browsing behaviour), and situational context (e.g. product inventory at local stores). Best of all, an orchestration layer intelligently selects which recommendation strategy to use in real-time, ensuring that the most effective approach is always employed. This results in increased conversions, enhanced customer experiences, and maximized revenue foronline retailers.

A: Call SetTransform before or after your World::step call. Make sure it's not inside a Box2D callback, as those happen inside the step, during which the World is locked and changes to bodies are restricted. To ensure you avoid triggering an `IsLocked()` assertion error when using `SetTransform`, follow these steps: 1. Outside of Step: Apply transformations outside your call to `world->Step()`. If you need to set a new position for the bullet during each frame, do so either before or after calling `world->Step(deltaTime)`. 2. Avoid Callbacks and Contacts: Do not modify body properties within Box2D callbacks (such as contact listeners) because these are invoked while the world is locked inside of step operations. 3. Synchronization with Physics Step: - If your game logic requires setting a position based on other dynamic factors, ensure this happens in synchronization with when you update and simulate physics. The introduction of checkpoint inhibitors targeting PD-1/Pd-L1 pathways in lymphoma patients is another major advancement. These immunotherapy approaches aim to reinvigorate the body's immune system, allowing it to better recognize and attack cancer cells that have managed to evade detection by conventional means. Moreover, targeted therapies are also being developed for specific genetic abnormalities found within aggressive lymphomas' genomes (like mutations in MYD88 or BTK). These drugs specifically target these aberrant pathways without affecting normal cell functions. A panel of stakeholders (medical school deans, faculty, medical students, and administrators) completed a Web-based instrument to create a linear scale of social mission content (SMC scale), scoring the degree to which medical school mission statements reflect the social mission of medical education to address inequities. The SMC scale and targeted medical school outputs were analyzed via OLS regression, controlling for allopathic/osteopathic and public/private school designation. The medical school outputs of interest included percent physician output in primary care specialties (family medicine, pediatrics, and general internal medicine), as well as percent physician output in designated Health Professional Shortage Areas (HPSA) and Medically Underserved Areas/Populations (MUA/P). SMC scale was a statistically significant, positive predictor of the percent of physician graduates entering primary care (β=2.05, p<0.05). Additionally, the SMC scale was also a significant predictor of the percent of physician graduates practicing in HPSAs (β=1.53, p<0.01) and MUAs/MUPs (β=1.32, p<0.05). In accordance with the mission of the NHLBI to improve the care of patients with lung disease, this proposal focuses on the role of two molecules, syndecan-1 and keratinocyte chemokine (KC), as modulators of this inflammatory respose in pulmonary conditions through their ability both directly enhance immune cell activation/recruitment while indirectly influencing epithelial repair. Syndecans are a family heparan sulfate proteoglycans present on the surface many mammalian cells including bronchial epithelia which line conducting airways within lung parenchyma; here they act as mediators for various physiological processes like tissue regeneration or inflammation among others via regulation cellular signaling pathways involving growth factors chemokines etcetera. One member - syndecan-1 (SDC‐ 4) has been shown by us previously to be critical regulator neutrophil transendothelial migration across vascular endothelial barrier during acute lung injury models using gene deficient mice lacking it entirely showing significantly reduced number migrating cells into alveolar compartments following intratracheal lipopolysaccharide challenge compared wild type controls under same conditions. In addition, our recent data implicates SDC-1 involvement in epithelial repair mechanisms after tissue damage occurs due excessive inflammation caused bacterial/viral infections or environmental toxins since we observed decreased levels soluble form sSdc‐4 were associated with enhanced permeability barrier function impaired migration wound closure studies cultured bronchial epithelia cells upon LPS stimulation. texts.full_text: Hunger and Thirst with Empty Bowls 6th grade students at Chevy Chase Elementary School in Maryland held an Empty Bowl fundraiser for Amman Imman and raised $900! These students are part of the Middle Years Program (MYP) of the International Baccalaureate. In conjunction with their studies of West African music, art and folk tales, the students heard Amman Imman's presentation about water scarcity in the Azawak. The stories inspired the students; they continued to talk about it after the presentation and they wanted to help. In art, the students made beautiful clay bowls that symbolized their assertion that no one should go without food or clean drinking water. The Empty Bowl fundraiser was held on December 10th in honor of International Human Rights Day (December 9-11). The event featured student performances and a silent auction. Le Grand Château This year, Too Faced pulled out all the stops with their palette presentation! The Le Grand Château houses (and is quite literally a house) three eye and face palettes and a deluxe size Better Than Sex mascara. The top of the house comes off and the the doors open to reveal the products inside. Even on the inside of the house the detail continues, as a cute fainting couch and window with a night scene of th glittering Eiffel Tower greets you. All in all there are 18 eyeshadows (6 matte, 10 shimmer and 2 glitter), one shimmering bronzer and two matte blushes. The palettes give a beautiful mix of warm, metallic and cool shadows and I love the easy wear-ability of the shadows – even Provence, the purple shade is soft enough to wear every day, and Fleur is a beautiful rose-gold which is perfect for the holidays. I like that the palettes have been split up, rather than one huge palette which makes it easier to grab one and go, however I wish they would have placed all of the eyeshadows in two palettes and put the two blushes and bronzer in a one because if you want to bring both a blush and the bronzer along you’ll have to grab at least two of the sets. I suppose you could gift each one of the palettes separately, but then that means you would be separating them from this adorable packaging (and seriously that would be soul-crushing!) For these reasons the invention can also involve a combination of factors which may, individually or in combination, represent the appropriate solutions for a given application. This disclosure is thus to be considered broad enough so that the various combinations and permeations of the techniques disclosed may be achieved. Undiscovered synergies may exist with other factors. Such factors may range from factors within the sorting or flow cytometer steps to those in the collection as well as insemination steps. At present, studies have been primarily achieved on bovine species, however, it is not believed that these techniques will be limited to such species or, for that matter to only sperm cells. It appears that the techniques used may have application beyond just sperm cells into areas which involve either sensitive items to be sorted or merely minimization of the impacts of the stresses of flow cytometry upon the item sorted. Interestingly, while the present invention takes an approach to minimize the impacts and stresses upon the sperm cells, others appear to have actually taken steps away from this direction by increasing pressures and demands for speed and other such performance. Essentially, the drive for low dose insemination and high speed processing may, in an individual or perhaps interrelated fashion have posed problems which limited one another. texts.full_text: itial swerve we had attempted—a desperate move that saved us from what could have been an even more fatal encounter with both water and vehicle. As soon they saw there was no escape via our side of this perilous ordeal – my Ferdinand (also known as Freddy), a swimmer who's worked in construction before decided to leap out the window right away. As he hit me on his way down I grabbed him and we rolled together. The vehicle continued its path into Claymore Springs with all five of us still inside at that point – only Ferdinand managed to escape but when it came our turn for impact everyone in there was flung about like rag dolls. As the van sank under water my friends and I were thrown out from an open door before being submerged into a river. The moment of truth had arrived - we needed instinct more than ever to fight against that current, but only one person could swim: Ferdinand – as he took us all in his arms like babies. texts.full_text: anada’s Aboriginal peoples still exists. In 2011, 27.1 per cent of Canada’s Aboriginal households were food insecure, as opposed to 11.5 per cent of non-Aboriginal households. A nutrition survey conducted in 2004 found that 33 per cent of off-reserve Aboriginal households were food insecure, compared to 9 per cent of other Canadians. In part, this food insecurity was attributable to higher rates of reliance on social assistance and higher rates of lone parenthood among Aboriginals than non-Aboriginals, but even controlling for these characteristics, the gap persists due to additional societal problems such as: 1. Historical trauma: The legacy of emigration, residential schools, and forced relocation has had a significant effect on cultural systems, economic practices, and social opportunities for Indigenous individuals. 2. Economic Barriers: many indigenous peoples reside in remote or mountainous regions where the price of food is considerably lower due to transportation costs. These areas also have limited job markets, leading to lower family taxes compared to rural centres. 3. Loss of Traditional Lands and Practices: The disruption of traditional hunting, fishing, and gathering methods has contributed to a dependence on store-bought meals, which are often more inexpensive and less nutritious than traditionally sourced meat. 4. Social Disparities: Aboriginal populations face higher rates of severe disorders such as diabetes and heart disease, which can be caused by inadequate health. Access to healthcare is therefore particularly limited in remote regions. 5. Socioeconomic inequality: despite attempts to address it, systemic racism continues to improve health levels, job opportunities, and housing quality for other Aboriginal peoples, contributing to ongoing food insecurity. 6. Climate Changes and Economic Degradation: These variables can impact the availability of traditional foods by destroying habitats and decreasing biodiversity in regions where Aboriginal peoples rely on natural resources. Also when determining for social status, these multifaceted issues contribute greatly to the lower rates of food poverty among Indigenous families compared to non-Aboriginal ones. Understanding this complex issue involves a comprehensive strategy that involves policy

texts.full_text: besides our own will only destroy us in the end. Biodiversity is essential to life and humans need animals in order to survive. Our treatment of creatures such as horses seems to indicate we’ve forgotten that, and for our own sake it’s time we set out to correct this. Purpose Horses and humans can get along in all sorts of ways. People such as veterans and those with autism have found special connections with hoses and this relationship has dramatically improved their lives. To be able to preserve and protect these animals, more people need to have these experiences. Our work with hoses allows people to connect personally with these animals, to see their beauty and to understand their desire to live. Having this experience is life-changing and perspective-altering and will inspire compassion towards these animals that perhaps had never been present before. Getting children involved in these events makes our impact even higher. They are the next generation and exposing them to this kind of compassion early on sets us up for a better world in the future. texts.full_text: olubilizers' on the membrane permeability of different compounds. This might be useful in selecting solubilizers for poorly water soluble compounds to be used for further in vivo studies. A diverse set of 20 drugs using UV-VIS detection were compared with data using LC/MS detection. A PAMPA screening method was designed which used solubilizers (Brij 35, Cremophor EL, ethanol, and Tween 80) for compounds with low aqueous solubility. The stability of the artificial membrane was determined using various solubilizer concentrations (0.1-5% w/v) to ensure the phospholipid membrane was not disrupted. Two compounds, amiodarone and miconazole, with low aqueous solubility yielding an undetected response in the PAMPA assay using UV-VIS detection were subjected to the different solubilizing agents and their PAMPA permeability was measured using LC/MS detection. Most of the compounds showed similar PAMPA permeability using the two detection systems. However, for compounds lacking a UV chromophore or with a low UV extinction coefficient, LC/MS was the detection method of choice for determination of PAMPA permeability values. LC/MS also gave reliable quantification data for compounds containing impurities, as well as compounds that were not stable during the assay. Although many solubilizers were found to interfere with UV-VIS detection, the LC/MS approach was applicable to determine the permeability values of compounds with normally low aqueous solubility.
